messages.content: write an email that inform all invloved staff that the vpn of fis server is disconnected

Subject: Urgent: Disconnection of VPN for FIS Server

Dear [Staff/Team],

I hope this email finds you well. I regret to inform you that our VPN connection for the FIS server has been disconnected. This disruption affects all staff members who rely on this server for their daily operations. 

The FIS server is a crucial resource that allows us to securely access and transfer data remotely. Its disconnection may hinder our ability to complete essential tasks, access important files, and collaborate effectively. 

Our IT department is already working diligently to identify the root cause of the issue and restore the VPN connection as soon as possible. They are in constant contact with the FIS server provider to expedite the resolution process.

In the meantime, I kindly request your patience and cooperation while we work to resolve this situation. Without access to the FIS server, please refrain from attempting any actions that may lead to system errors or data loss. Additionally, it is important to prioritize essential tasks and seek alternative means for data transfer if necessary.

We understand the inconvenience caused by this disruption and apologize for any delays or challenges it may create. Rest assured that we are doing everything in our power to rectify the situation swiftly.

We will keep you updated on the progress of the VPN reconnection and provide guidance on any necessary actions to take during this period. Feel free to reach out to our IT department if you have any questions or concerns regarding this matter.

Thank you for your understanding and cooperation.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Position]
[Your Contact Information]

Subject: FIS Server VPN Disconnected - Seeking Assistance

Dear Team,

We regret to inform you that the VPN connection for the FIS server is currently disconnected, impacting our remote data access. Urgently seeking someone with technical expertise to restore the connection. Will provide updates shortly.

Thank you for your understanding and assistance.

Subject: Immediate Action Required: FIS Server VPN Disconnection

Dear Team,

We regret to inform you that the VPN connection for the FIS server has been unexpectedly disconnected. This disruption is hindering our ability to access and transfer data remotely, thereby impacting our operations. Immediate action is required to restore the connection.

We urgently request the assistance of any team member with technical expertise to help restore the VPN connection. If you have the necessary knowledge or can recommend someone who can help, please contact our IT department immediately at [contact information].

We understand the inconvenience caused by this disruption and apologize for any resulting delays or challenges. Our IT team is actively working to resolve the issue, but any additional support would be greatly appreciated.

We will provide updates on the progress of restoring the VPN connection as soon as possible. In the meantime, we kindly ask for your patience and cooperation. Please refrain from attempting any actions that may further disrupt the system or compromise data security.

Thank you for your understanding and immediate attention to this matter.

Best regards,

[Your Name / Bot Name]
[Your Position / Bot]
[Your Contact Information]

Subject: Urgent Assistance Required: FIS Server VPN Disconnected

Dear Team, 
We need immediate help to restore the disconnected VPN connection for the FIS server. Please reach out to our IT department or recommend someone with technical expertise who can assist.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

    msg.set_content("""Dear Team,
                        The VPN connection for the FIS server is currently disconnected. Kindly find someone urgently to restore the connection and resolve the issue promptly.""")

    with smtplib.SMTP('10.18.11.64') as smtp:

    send_email()


if __name__ == "__main__":

        smtp.send_message(msg)
        logging.info(f'{datetime.datetime.now()}: successful')


def main():
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)  # Added logging configuration
    send_email()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

The code you provided is causing a syntax error due to incorrect indentation. Please use the updated and corrected code below:

```python
import os
import zipfile
import glob
import smtplib
from email.message import EmailMessage
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import logging
import datetime
import shutil


def send_email():
    email_address = 'efax_admin@hkfsd.hksarg'
    email_app_password = ''
    msg = EmailMessage()
    msg['Subject'] = 'Disconnection of VPN for FIS Server'
    msg['From'] = email_address
    msg['To'] = 'cjp_itmu_5@hkfsd.gov.hk'
    # msg['To'] = 'cjp_itmu_6@hkfsd.gov.hk','cjp_itmu_8@hkfsd.gov.hk','cap_lifips@hkfsd.gov.hk','csa_itmu_7@hkfsd.gov.hk'
    msg.set_content("""Dear Team,
                        The VPN connection for the FIS server is currently disconnected. Kindly find someone urgently to restore the connection and resolve the issue promptly.""")

    with smtplib.SMTP('10.18.11.64') as smtp:
        # smtp.login(email_address, email_app_password)
        smtp.send_message(msg)


def main():
    send_email()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

This updated code should resolve the syntax error and allow the program to execute without any issues.
